  • Automatic state transitions — @TO: #Finished updates the entity after your action returns
  • History tracking — @TO: $flow.previous restores the previous state (perfect for a snake bite that temporarily blocks a player)
  • Fiori integration — buttons in the UI are automatically enabled/disabled based on the current status

Snake & Ladder maps directly onto this model: a player's turn is a state machine, and every square is a potential transition trigger.

Challenge Format

Each week a blog post is published on SAP Community with the task, hints, and code guidance. Submit your answer as a reply comment on that week's blog post.

Week Dates Title Core Topic Blog Post

16–12 JulModel the BoardCDS entities, status enum, @flow.statuslink
213–19 JulRoll & MoveBound actions, @from / @to transitionsTBD
320–26 JulMultiplayer & EventsTurn management, CAP events, snake/ladder logicTBD
427–31 JulFiori UI & CAP MCPBoard UI, MCP serverTBD

Prerequisites

  • Node.js 20+
  • @sap/cds-dk installed globally: npm install -g @sap/cds-dk
  • VS Code with the SAP CDS Language Support extension
  • Basic familiarity with CAP (entities, services, cds watch)

No prior knowledge of CAP status flows is required — Week 1 introduces everything from scratch.

The Board

The game uses a classic 10×10 board (squares 1–100). Key squares:

Ladders (climb up)

Bottom Top Effect

238Big early boost
831Good start
1526Small step up
2142Mid-board jump
2884The long ladder — almost wins it
3644Short gain
5167Solid advance
7191Late-game boost
7898So close to victory

Single-Headed Snakes (slide back)

Head Tail Severity

997Devastating — one square from the finish
7620Major setback
4318Mid-game punisher

Double-Headed Snakes (two biting ends, one shared tail)

These special snakes have two heads on different squares that share a single tail. Landing on either head square sends you to the same tail square.

Head 1 Head 2 Shared Tail

897250
546334
40473

The double-headed snake maps to CAP's @from: [#Head1, #Head2] — the same transition triggered by multiple entry states.

Reusable Board UI

A ready-made HTML/JS board will be provided later. Participants plug in their CAP service URL and see the live board — no frontend coding required for the challenge.

Resources

    • CAP Status Transition Flows Documentation
    • CAP Node.js Documentation
    • Initial Setup

entry.title: E2E Key-User Extensibility: A comprehensive guide for Functional Consultants
entry.content:
Introduction:
Key-user extensibility is the capability provided in SAP Fiori apps with UI adaptation to customize, configure and extend fiori apps as per business requirements without deep technical knowledge or developer's envolvement. This is specifically meant for Functional consultants but teams often pushes it to developer's bucket.
This comprehensive guide will help you customize your apps, reducing reliance on development team.


Pre-requisite:
To enable key-user extensbility, you must perform below activities with the help of technical team. 

  1. ATO setup: Adaptation Transport Organizer(ATO) setup is required to transport changes done inside Fiori apps. Local Package will capture your changes and you can move it to your package later for transport movement. This setup is thoroughly explained in below SAP note,
    Snote: 2807979

  2. Role assignment: Assign below roles to functional consultants whom you want to make key-users:

    1. SAP_UI_FLEX_KEY_USER - To enable "Adapt UI" option

    2. SAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC - To get access to relevant apps for key-user extensibility

  3. Apps enablement: Check access to below Fiori apps and register/enable associated Odata services. These apps should run properly to get fruitful results.
    1. Custom Fields and Logic (App ID: F1590)

    2. Configure Software Packages (App ID: F1418)

    3. Register Extensions for Transport (App ID: F1589)


Key-user extensibility:

  1. Visit "Custom Fields and Logic" app and click on "+" icon to add a new field as per business requirement.
    e.g., I am adding "Interest Bearing" as a dropdown option of "Yes(Y)/No(N)" inside "Manage Bank Accounts" fiori app.

    NOTE: You must select right Business Context to get the field in relevant Fiori app.

  2. Once added, "Edit" it and go to "User interfaces" tab. Here, perform "Enable Usage(1)" for relevant app's backend and then SAVE(2) and PUBLISH(3).
     
  3.  On succesful completion and publishing, your new field will be marked as published. Additionally, you will be able to verify this field as a new "Append structure" in backend tables.



  4. Additionally, to transfer this newly added field and relevant changes, visit application "Configure Software Packages". Add Registration(1) to add your Z-package and Select Workbench Transport(2) to capture field changes into this transport.

     

  5. Furthremore, to reassign your newly added custom field to your registered Z-package and transport, go to application "Register Extensions for Transport".
    Here, select your field and click on "Reassign to Package".

    After reassignment, it will appear like this.



  6. Now, Visit the application's screen where you want to add this new field. In our case, it's "Manage Bank Accounts". Click on Profile icon and select "Adapt UI" and click "Ok" on the message pop-up if it appears.




  7. UI adaptation mode will appear as shown below. And if you move cursor over this screen, it will highlight over sections, blocks and fields where you would like to make a change as per business requirement.

     

  8. As I want to add a new field in a section e.g. in "Entity Details". I will select the section and click on "+" icon as show. All fields from Business Context will appear and you can search and add your field e.g. "Interest Bearing".



  9. The new field will appear on screen as show below. And then you have to click SAVE(1) and ACTIVATE NEW VERSION(2) button.



  10. After saving new version e.g. 1.0.1, you can click on Transport icon to capture such changes inside a transport. You can select Workbench TR for Cross-client changes and Customizing TR for client-specific changes.



  11. Finally, click on 'X' to exit from UI Adaptation mode. A pop-up message might appear, click "OK" and come out of it.


By following all the above steps, we have successfully added a new field using key-user extensibility and captured these changes into transport for movement to next system in the landscape.

 

Thanks for reading the blog, comments are always welcome.
Happy Learning!
